title: Flight path shake-up plan for 'outdated' 1950s airspace over Scotland

sections.heading: What happened
sections.content: A consultation has opened into plans designed to reduce noise and cut emissions around Edinburgh and Glasgow airports.

sections.heading: Key details
sections.content: Flight path shake-up plan for 'outdated' 1950s airspace over Scotland The plans are designed to reduce noise and cut emissions during take offs and landings

sections.heading: Why it matters
sections.content: Edinburgh and Glasgow airports could see the biggest shake-up of Scotland's airspace since the 1950s under new flight path proposals. A public consultation has opened into the plans, which are designed to reduce noise and cut emissions during take offs and landings.

sourceReferences.citationText: serper-scottish-aviation – Flight path shake-up plan for 'outdated' 1950s airspace over Scotland
